title: Numerical Linear Algebra and Optimization · Model Repair Bay
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving large constrained computational models. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a reproducible numerical linear optimization computational study and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate direct iterative solvers in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive matrix conditioning in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute gradient methods in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Automatic Differentiation evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Automatic Differentiation assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Communicate automatic differentiation in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: A model passes through (1, 5) and (5, 17). Calculate its average rate of change.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (17 − 5) / (5 − 1).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (17 − 5) / (5 − 1).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Automatic Differentiation applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Communicate automatic differentiation in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
